quick_facts.label: Core function
quick_facts.value: A CSSD technician supports the hospital sterile-supply workflow: used instruments and reusable items are received, cleaned, packed, sterilised, stored and dispatched for clinical use.

quick_facts.label: Patient-safety role
quick_facts.value: CSSD/SSD serves hospital areas including the operation theatre and is identified in national infection-prevention guidance as important to infection prevention and patient safety.

quick_facts.label: Common entry pattern
quick_facts.value: Entry requirements are employer-specific. Official hospital advertisements have used 10+2 Science plus a CSSD diploma/certificate and relevant hospital CSSD experience, while other advertisements accept 10+2 Science with a post-qualification internship or experience preference.

quick_facts.label: Technical focus
quick_facts.value: Training content can include operating steam and gas sterilizers, ultrasonic cleaners and washer-disinfectors; monitoring sterilizer efficacy and records; infection prevention; and biomedical-waste management.

quick_facts.label: Work setting
quick_facts.value: The role is hospital-based and interfaces with operation theatres, ICUs, emergency rooms, wards, nursing, infection-control and maintenance services.

sections.heading: Responsibilities and accountability
paragraphs: The technician’s work is a controlled reprocessing and supply cycle rather than direct surgical assistance. Duties described by the Directorate General of Health Services include receiving used instruments and reusable items, cleaning them to hospital policy, packing, loading and unloading sterilizers, holding sterile articles in the sterile zone, maintaining inventories, and dispatching sterile material to the required areas.

A technician is also responsible for keeping sterilizers functional, supporting regular equipment calibration, applying quality control to the sterilisation process, following universal precautions, and complying with biomedical-waste requirements. National infection-prevention guidance additionally identifies the need to monitor sterilisation conditions and processes, maintain records of sterilizer runs, and keep clean and dirty areas appropriately separated.
bullets: Process and prepare instruments, linen, dressings and other approved reusable medical devices for sterilisation.
Operate and monitor sterilisation and cleaning equipment according to local SOPs and manufacturer instructions.
Maintain traceability, inventory and sterilizer-cycle records; identify or report equipment defects through the hospital’s process.
Follow infection-prevention, universal-precaution and biomedical-waste procedures.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: There is no single national entry rule evidenced across employers in the sources reviewed; applicants should treat each recruitment notice as controlling. A Tata Memorial Centre advertisement for Technician (CSSD) required 12th-standard Science, a six-month or one-year CSSD diploma from a recognised institution, and one year’s CSSD experience in a large hospital. A separate 2024 Tata Memorial Centre deployment advertisement listed full-time 12th Science with at least 50% marks and treated one year of post-qualification CSSD internship/experience as preferred.

A government-recognised Kerala vocational curriculum illustrates a skills-training route: its CSSD Technician module specifies 270 hours and covers sterile-supply services, microbiology, sterilisation and equipment handling. Delhi’s Rajiv Gandhi Super Speciality Hospital has also advertised a more experience-led route for its combined Technician Grade II CSSD/OT/CTVS post, accepting 10+2 Science plus a relevant course and five years’ relevant experience, or a B.Sc. plus two years’ relevant experience.
bullets: Complete the education and any CSSD/operation-theatre training specifically required in the target employer’s notice.
Build supervised CSSD experience or internship exposure in a hospital, particularly where a notice requires or prefers it.
Check whether the post is a dedicated CSSD role or a combined technician cadre, because qualifications and experience thresholds can differ.
For public-sector recruitment, verify the current advertisement, reservation/age conditions and any examination or skill-test requirements directly with the recruiting organisation.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: Technical competence centres on cleaning, disinfection, packing and sterilisation; choosing and controlling appropriate sterilisation conditions; equipment operation; and quality monitoring. The Kerala curriculum includes steam and gas sterilizers, ultrasonic cleaners and washer-disinfectors, preventive maintenance, microbiological reports, water-quality checks and sterilisation-quality assessment.

The role also requires disciplined infection-control behaviour. National guidance calls for knowledge of medical-device sterilisation and monitoring procedures, while the DGHS duty list explicitly includes universal precautions, quality control and biomedical-waste compliance. Record keeping, careful separation of clean and contaminated workflow, communication with users of the service, and escalation of defects are practical operational skills.
bullets: Instrument and device reprocessing; pack preparation; sterile storage and issue.
Sterilizer, washer-disinfector and cleaner operation, routine checks and fault escalation.
Physical, chemical and/or microbiological quality-monitoring practices as specified by hospital policy.
Infection prevention, universal precautions, biomedical-waste handling and documentation.
Inventory control and clear coordination with OT, wards, nursing, infection-control and maintenance teams.

sections.heading: Workplace and working relationships
paragraphs: CSSD is a central hospital support service, serving areas that include the OT and linking with other departments that require sterile reusable devices. National guidance identifies collaboration with the hospital infection-control committee and relevant departments on cleaning, decontamination and sterilisation policies; it also identifies communication with the IPC team, nursing, OT, transport, pharmacy and maintenance services.

The workplace must maintain distinct contaminated and sterile workflows. The DGHS duty list refers to receiving used material, washing and packing it, sterilizing it, storing it in a sterile zone and dispatching it; national guidance likewise calls for separation of clean and dirty areas. This means the role is procedure-driven and typically requires consistent PPE, hygiene, documentation and turnaround discipline.
bullets: Central sterile supply department within hospitals and large clinical institutes.
Regular service interfaces with operation theatres, ICUs, emergency rooms, wards and clinical support teams.
Work spans contaminated receiving/cleaning functions and controlled sterile storage/issue functions under local workflow controls.

sections.heading: Progression possibilities
paragraphs: Progression can move toward senior CSSD technical and supervisory responsibility. In the DGHS duty allocation, the Senior Technician (CSSD) supervises CSSD equipment and supplies, deploys and supervises technical staff, and ensures calibration, quality control, universal precautions and biomedical-waste compliance.

A related progression example appears in a Tata Memorial Centre advertisement: its Scientific Assistant (CSSD) role required a B.Sc. with at least 50% marks and two years’ experience as a CSSD technician in the sterilisation department of a large hospital/institute. Actual titles, qualifications, pay scales and promotion rules vary by employer and recruitment cadre.
bullets: CSSD Technician → Senior Technician (CSSD) or equivalent supervisory technician post.
With the qualifications and experience specified by an employer, movement to scientific-assistant or other sterilisation-service roles may be possible.
Quality assurance, equipment oversight, inventory control and staff coordination are relevant experience areas for advancement.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Pay is highly employer-, location-, contract- and experience-dependent, so a single national salary figure would be misleading. As dated examples only, a Tata Memorial Centre 2022 walk-in advertisement listed ₹16,042 per month for Technician (CSSD), while a Tata Memorial Centre deployment advertisement dated March 2024 listed ₹18,500 per month for one Technician (CSSD) post. These were vacancy-specific advertised amounts, not current market rates or a guaranteed pay range.

Candidates should compare the current recruitment notice’s pay basis—regular pay level versus consolidated contractual remuneration—along with benefits, shift/on-call requirements, location and eligibility conditions before treating any figure as comparable.
bullets: Use dated vacancy advertisements only as context, not as a national benchmark.
Confirm whether the stated amount is consolidated pay or a regular government pay level.
Recheck the live notice before applying, since compensation and terms can change by recruitment cycle.
